Senator David Roberti
PRESIDENT PRO TEMPORE, CALIFORNIA STATE SENATE
May 10, 1990
Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTIEES EC}iI,ITSSION
428 rtJrr St., Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 958L4
REs Request for Formal Written Advice
Dear Ms. Donovan:
I am an elected state officer. 
I am the legislator who carried the
legislation which placed Proposition LO7 on the June 5 state
baIIot. 
Proposition 1-O7 is a bond measure for funds to assist in
relieving the problems of the homeless in california.
I control my own conmittee, House the Homeless, which is supporting
the baIlot measure. I am also on the honorary fundraising
committee for HOMEPAC, a sponsored committee of the California
Center on Housing Priorities, 
which is a general purpose ballot
measure committee. HOMEPAC is currently working for passage of
Proposition Lo7.
I wish to avoid any issues of involvement of my State Senate staff
with respect to the bond measure campaign. Hence, in order to
handle the cornrnunications with HOMEPAC without using Senate staff ,
f would like to have my conirolied committee, House the ilomel-ess,
contract with a person on a part-time basis, to handle those
communications. fnis would involve such things as scheduling for
fund-raising events. 
The expected amount of time would be a
maximum of i5 hours per month. The contract would expressly state
that this person would not in any way direct, instruct or otherwise
control the activities of HOMEPIC's campaign to pass Proposition
r_07.
My question is whether, under the foregoing facts, I am correct in
;y irnderstanding that this would not make HOMEPAC a committee
c6ntrolled by me-. I partjcutarly base that understanding on your
previous aariice letter to Mr. Patrick, oD behalf of senator
Boatwright.
Reply to: P.O. Box 2856, Los Angeles, CA 9OO28
NOT PRINTED AT PUBLIC EXPENSE
@ror


Kathryn Donovan
Uay 10, 1990
Page 2
I realize you and your staff are very busy, however, because of
the closeness of the June 5 election and the need for HoMEPAC to
engage in active fund raising betlreen now and then, r vrould
greatly appreciate receiving interim telephone advice as soon as
practicable so that r may Proceed.
5ffi&be-
DAVID ROBERTI
President pro Tempore
DR: cmrmd

sections.question: Whether, under the foregoing facts, I am correct in my understanding that this would not make HOMEPAC a committee controlled by me. I particularly base that understanding on your previous advice letter to Mr. Patrick, on behalf of Senator Boatwright.
sections.question_synthetic: Senator Roberti seeks confirmation that hiring a part-time contractor through his controlled committee (House the Homeless) to handle communications with HOMEPAC, a sponsored committee supporting Proposition 107, would not result in HOMEPAC being considered a committee controlled by him.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from Senator Roberti to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION or ADVICE section is present in this document. The letter references a prior FPPC advice letter to Mr. Patrick on behalf of Senator Boatwright as supporting authority.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether, under the foregoing facts, I am correct in my understanding that this would not make HOMEPAC a committee controlled by me. I particularly base that understanding on your previous advice letter to Mr. Patrick, on behalf of Senator Boatwright.

embedding.summary: Senator David Roberti requests formal written advice from the FPPC regarding whether contracting a part-time person through his controlled committee (House the Homeless) to handle communications with HOMEPAC would make HOMEPAC a controlled committee. He cites time constraints due to the approaching June 5 election and requests interim telephone advice.
